channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Chisholm ejected for arguing called 3rd strike during Yankees' 6-3 win over Rays
item.description: Jazz Chisholm Jr. was ejected from the New York Yankees’ 6-3 victory over the Tampa Bay Rays, then appeared to violate Major League Baseball’s social media policy when he posted in-game criticism online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yan Liu, Ashleigh Buhai share lead at 63 in the LPGA Tour's JM Eagle LA Championship
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Yan Liu birdied the final three holes in chilly conditions Thursday afternoon for a 9-under 63 and a share of the first-round lead with Ashleigh Buhai in the LPGA Tour's JM Eagle LA Championship.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bill Belichick nabs 4-star receiver for UNC who was going to play for Deion Sanders at Colorado
item.description: Bill Belichick and UNC have brought in four-star receiver Adrian Wilson, who entered the transfer portal Wednesday after previously committing to Colorado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says he's not holding teams hostage as he ponders his future in the NFL
item.description: Aaron Rodgers is in no rush to make a decision about his future. The free-agent quarterback said during an appearance on “The Pat McAfee Show” on Thursday that he is “not holding anybody hostage" as he weighs whether to return for a 21st season. Rodgers was released by the New York Jets in March.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man United seals spectacular comeback to beat Lyon 5-4 and advance to Europa League semifinals
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Manchester United’s season isn’t done yet. On a night of high drama at Old Trafford, the troubled Premier League club put its domestic problems to one side by sealing a spectacular comeback to beat Lyon 5-4 and advance to the semifinals of the Europa League.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: PGA star signs woman's leg after hitting it with errant tee shot
item.description: Billy Horschel found himself in an awkward moment Thursday at the RBC Heritage. He signed a woman's leg after he hit it with a tee shot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luka Doncic eager to start playing big games for Lakers, starting in playoff date with Timberwolves
item.description: EL SEGUNDO, Calif. (AP) — Luka Doncic has been with the Los Angeles Lakers for 2 1/2 months now, and he's feeling fairly comfortable in his new home. He is building chemistry with his new teammates, who are getting used to his brilliant passes, prolific scoring outbursts and fiery competitiveness.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Aaron Rodgers rips Jets new head coach Aaron Glenn, reveals details of contentious final meeting
item.description: Aaron Rodgers' didn't hold back on detailing the contentious final meeting he had with New York Jets head coach Aaron Glenn during the offseason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nats pitcher Jorge López suspended 3 games for throwing at Pittsburgh's Andrew McCutchen's head
item.description: Washington Nationals pitcher Jorge López has been suspended three games and fined for throwing at Pittsburgh's Andrew McCutchen, Major League Baseball announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY Rangers' Panarin, MSG made settlement payments after employee sexual assault allegations: report
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The NHL said the New York Rangers informed the league last year they were looking into sexual assault allegations by a team employee against player Artemi Panarin after a report detailing the situation and settlement payments made to the woman surfaced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NCAA panel gives final OK to rule designed to discourage football players from faking injuries
item.description: The NCAA Playing Rules Oversight Panel gave final approval to a rule designed to discourage football players from faking injuries to stop the game clock.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The final four: What to know about the Champions League semifinals
item.description: Four teams are left in the Champions League and each has a quest for a rare European title. Arsenal meets Paris Saint-Germain in a semifinal between teams who have never lifted the iconic trophy.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Herro goes from raising eyebrows to turning heads as Heat knock out Bulls in Play-In Tournament
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Tyler Herro went from raising eyebrows with a shot he missed to turning heads one week later with all the baskets he made. Both times, he did it for Miami in Chicago.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Potential No. 1 pick Cam Ward names several Titans players as best in league
item.description: Potential No. 1 pick Cam Ward seems like he has an idea which team will select him next week in the NFL Draft after naming several Titans as top players in the league.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top WNBA draft pick Paige Bueckers announces investment stake in Unrivaled league
item.description: Unrivaled, a 3-on-3 pro women’s basketball league made it’s highly anticipated debut this past winter and was co-founded by WNBA stars by Breanna Stewart and Napheesa Collier.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Caitlin Clark not included on Time Magazine list of most influential people, prompting questions by fans
item.description: Caitlin Clark was not included on Time Magazine's list of The 100 Most Influential People of 2025, and many fans questioned how the decision was made.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No comeback for Real Madrid as Arsenal is back in Champions League semifinals
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Arsenal defeated defending champion Real Madrid 2-1 on Wednesday to reach the Champions League semifinals for the first time since 2009 in a match where VAR played a key role in two penalty decisions.
item.dc:creator: TALES AZZONI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cubs bullpen works 5 shutout innings, Hoerner's go-ahead triple in 10th hands Padres first home loss, 2-1
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — The San Diego Padres hadn't loss a home game yet on the young season. Until Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors edge Grizzlies in Play-In Tournament thriller, clinch playoff spot
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors are officially going to the 2025 NBA Playoffs after a nail-biting 119-116 win over the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday night in San Francisco. The victory sets Golden State (48-34) up with a first-round matchup against the No.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Frank Nazar scores 48 seconds into OT to give the Blackhawks a 4-3 win over the Senators
item.description: Frank Nazar scored 48 seconds into overtime to give the Chicago Blackhawks a 4-3 victory over the Ottawa Senators on Tuesday night in their season finale.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Cubs demote Shaw, Pearson to minors, call up Little, Palencia
item.description: The Chicago Cubs have demoted infield prospect Matt Shaw and relief pitcher Nate Pearson to Triple-A and recalled pitchers Luke Little and Daniel Palencia from the Iowa Cubs, while reinstating Vidal Bruján and moving relief pitcher Eli Morgan to the 15-day IL with a right elbow impingement.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Warriors host Memphis Grizzlies in NBA play-in game Tuesday at Chase Center
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (BCN) -- The Golden State Warriors missed a chance to clinch a National Basketball Association playoff spot in their last regular season game on Sunday but will get another chance when they host a play-in game Tuesday against the Memphis Grizzlies at Chase Center in San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Bay City News, KRON4

item.title: Prosecutor says whether Gaudreaus drank before deaths isn't relevant
item.description: According to the defense, the Gaudreaus had blood-alcohol levels of .129 or above, higher than the .08 legal limit in New Jersey and the .087 that police recorded for Sean Higgins.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Warriors' playoff seeding set after loss against Clippers in regular-season finale
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors' playoff path is set. They will play in the NBA Play-In Tournament following a home overtime loss to the Los Angeles Clippers in Sunday's regular-season finale. With the loss, the Warriors will face the Memphis Grizzlies on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Garrett Crochet dazzles in return to South Side, takes no-no into the 8th in Red Sox victory
item.description: Garrett Crochet returned to the South Side with a vengeance, throwing seven and one-third innings and striking out eleven, but the Red Sox won the game 3-1 to avoid a sweep.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Utah scores four power play goals in 5-3 victory
item.description: DALLAS (AP) - Logan Cooley had a goal and two assists to lead the Utah Hockey Club to a 5-3 win against the Dallas Stars at American Airlines Center on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Taylor gets 3 hits as the White Sox stop 8-game slide by routing the Red Sox 11-1
item.description: Michael A. Taylor had three hits and scored three times, and the Chicago White Sox stopped an eight-game slide by routing the sloppy Boston Red Sox 11-1 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
